message:
feat(fcp-core): add ManifestSignature enum + pin SchemaVersion (ManifestVersion) compatibility relation

Lands two related pinning artifacts that close the next pair of beads
in the current sweep — flywheel_connectors-8tf1r ("ManifestSignature
serde tag") and flywheel_connectors-3xfmr ("SchemaVersion
compatibility").

Production change (crates/fcp-core/src/connector_artifacts.rs):

- New `pub enum ManifestSignature` with three variants — Ed25519,
  RsaPss, EcdsaP256 — for the supported manifest-signature
  algorithms. Derives Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash,
  Serialize, Deserialize so the tag can be hashed for registry
  lookup, compared by value, and serialized to JSON / CBOR. Lives
  next to the existing SIGNED_MANIFEST_SIGNATURE_CONTEXT constant
  and the SignedManifest envelope so the surface stays cohesive.
- Re-exported via the crate root so tests can `use
  fcp_core::ManifestSignature` directly.

Pinning test (crates/fcp-core/tests/manifest_signature_serde_tags.rs,
new):

- ManifestSignatureCase fixture binds each variant to its canonical
  JSON tag and the deterministic CBOR hex byte sequence the
  ciborium encoder emits.
- Asserts JSON round-trip (the on-the-wire tag is exactly the
  expected string) and CBOR round-trip (the bytes are exactly the
  pinned hex). Adding a variant means appending one row; renaming
  one fails this test loudly.

Pinning test (crates/fcp-core/tests/schema_version_compatibility_relation.rs,
new):

- Header records the bead-text vs. type-name reconciliation
  ("SchemaVersion" in the bead → ManifestVersion / ConnectorVersion
  in the codebase), pointing at connector_artifacts.rs:17 for the
  underlying type.
- Pins the relation properties of
  `ManifestVersion::is_compatible_with` that the existing
  manifest_version_compatibility.rs doesn't enumerate explicitly —
  reflexivity (every version is compatible with itself), behavior
  across the major boundary, and the Display ↔ FromStr / serde
  round-trip on the underlying ManifestVersion struct.

No effect on existing call sites — both additions are net-new public
surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
